steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content: The contrast word “Nonetheless” followed by the example of “the third most commonly used password”—“123456789”—reveals what people actually choose, despite expert advice for complicated passwords.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
Popular captures what people actually choose, as the example of “the third most commonly used password” demonstrates—people select convenient passwords over secure ones.

The other choices either lack connection to user behavior or contradict the convenience-focused point.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
